_comment: The Riverbank: a long, linear walk down a riverside. You reach it through a portal in the Vale and step back through the portal at the start. Scattered along the bank and its shallows are rocks you can examine — ten hide little salamanders (the goal), a few hide other small finds that don't count, and many hide nothing. The hunt is laid out fresh and RANDOM every visit (see world_controller + SalamanderHunt), so 'rocks' here are just candidate positions; what's under each is decided at load. Find all ten and a second portal home shimmers open nearby. Coordinates are world-space pixels; colors are [r,g,b] 0..1. Layout: woods along the top (y ~ -210), the dry bank in the middle, the waterline near y~30, and the river filling the lower screen.
_regions_comment: Two stretches of the bank, for the companion's new-area discovery bond and a faint underfoot tint. Same scheme as the Vale (world_id:region_id).
_bounds_comment: A wide strip, taller than the 360px viewport so the camera never reveals void at the edges: woods + dry bank up top, river along the bottom. Long to walk down; the camera tracks sideways as you go.
_river_comment: The river: a long water band filling the lower part of the world, drawn by world_art with a lit bank-side edge and slow ripples drifting downstream. Presentation-only — it doesn't block movement this rung, so you can wade to the waterline rocks; the bounds keep you in the world.
_trees_comment: A treeline of woods backing the bank along the top edge, plus a few scattered mid-bank for depth, so the place feels enclosed on the land side and open onto the water.
_rocks_comment: Candidate rock positions along the waterline and into the shallows. Contents are assigned randomly at load by SalamanderHunt — there must be at least goal.count + goal.decoy_count of them.
_goal_comment: The hunt: hide `count` salamanders (these count) and `decoy_count` decoys (these don't) among the rocks; the rest are empty. Decoys are tagged so the companion appraises them too (see companion.json 'appraisal'). flip_budget caps how many rocks you may turn over before the hunt ends (re-tapping a flipped rock is free) — so the skill is reading your companion's 'tell' (companion.json 'detector') to spend flips on real salamanders, not flipping every rock. Find all `count` within budget for a win (flawless = a 'perfect hunt'); run out and every rock flips face-up dimmed so you see what you missed, then the way home opens and you can come back for a fresh shuffle. Set flip_budget to 0 to disable the budget (unlimited flips).

decoys.label: a snail curled in its spiral shell

_companion_comment: Per-world companion tuning, merged over companion.json by world_controller. The riverbank is a focused 'help me search' activity, so unlike the open Vale we keep the companion close at hand REGARDLESS of bond — both to quiet its wandering and, crucially, so it's near enough to the rocks to point out salamanders (the detector only fires when the COMPANION is near a hidden one). follow_near_low: trail near (300) even when fresh, instead of the Vale's wide 1200 berth — the single biggest lever. follow_eager_low/high + follow_leash: bid harder to stay at your side. wander_score_low/high + roam_radius: roam less, and stay close when it does. Tune these to taste; remove the block to fall back to global behavior.
_portals_comment: Doorways between worlds (walk through them). The entry portal here returns to the Vale, landing the player beside the Vale's portal. A second 'all ten found' portal is created at runtime nearby when the hunt completes.
